req.title: Export employee directory
req.statement: An hr-admin shall export the employee directory as CSV with PII redacted for employees who opted out of public directory.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three distinct test dimensions: (1) a happy-path export by a valid hr-admin that returns a CSV with PII redacted for opted-out employees (EP positive), (2) a forbidden access attempt by a non-admin role (NEG negative), (3) a rate-limit boundary triggered by exceeding the allowed request quota (BVA boundary), (4) a decision-table case verifying that employees who have NOT opted out appear with full PII while opted-out employees are redacted in the same export (DT positive), and (5) a state-transition case covering the opt-out flag toggle: employee opts out then opts back in, verifying PII visibility changes accordingly (STC positive). Together these cases achieve EP, NEG, BVA, DT, and STC heuristic coverage for R-WEB-082.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_export_csv_pii_redacted_for_opted_out
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role hr-admin
At least one employee record has opted_out=true with known email
At least one employee record has opted_out=false with known email
actions: Issue GET /api/employees/export.csv with hr-admin bearer token
expected: HTTP 200 with Content-Type text/csv or application/octet-stream
opted-out employee row has PII fields (email, phone, address) replaced with redaction marker
non-opted-out employee row retains full PII values
CSV contains a header row with expected column names
tests.executable:
import csv, io
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import get_auth_token, get_opted_out_employee, get_opted_in_employee

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"
REDACT_MARKER = "REDACTED"


def test_hr_admin_export_csv_pii_redacted_for_opted_out(<http_client>):
    token = get_auth_token(role="hr-admin")
    opted_out = get_opted_out_employee()
    opted_in = get_opted_in_employee()

    resp = <http_client>.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    ct = resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "")
    assert "csv" in ct or "octet-stream" in ct

    reader = csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text))
    rows = {r["employee_id"]: r for r in reader}

    out_row = rows[opted_out["id"]]
    assert out_row["email"] == REDACT_MARKER
    assert out_row["phone"] == REDACT_MARKER

    in_row = rows[opted_in["id"]]
    assert in_row["email"] == opted_in["email"]
    assert in_row["phone"] == opted_in["phone"]

tests.name: test_non_admin_export_returns_403
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role employee (not hr-admin)
actions: Issue GET /api/employees/export.csv with employee-role bearer token
expected: Response body contains an error message indicating insufficient permissions
trace: R-WEB-082: error pathway 403 if not hr-admin
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import get_auth_token

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"


def test_non_admin_export_returns_403(<http_client>):
    token = get_auth_token(role="employee")

    resp = <http_client>.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body
    ct = resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "")
    assert "csv" not in ct

tests.name: test_export_rate_limit_returns_429_on_threshold_breach
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role hr-admin
Rate limit for /api/employees/export.csv is known (e.g. N requests per window)
Rate-limit counter for the hr-admin account is reset/fresh
actions: Issue exactly N successful GET requests to /api/employees/export.csv (at the limit)
Issue one additional GET request (N+1, exceeding the limit)
expected: First N requests each return HTTP 200
The (N+1)-th request returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
429 response includes Retry-After or X-RateLimit-Reset header
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import get_auth_token, get_rate_limit_threshold, reset_rate_limit

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"


def test_export_rate_limit_returns_429_on_threshold_breach(<http_client>):
    token = get_auth_token(role="hr-admin")
    limit = get_rate_limit_threshold(endpoint="/api/employees/export.csv")
    reset_rate_limit(token=token, endpoint="/api/employees/export.csv")

    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/employees/export.csv"

    for i in range(limit):
        r = <http_client>.get(url, headers=headers)
        assert r.status_code == 200, f"Request {i+1} unexpectedly failed: {r.status_code}"

    over_limit = <http_client>.get(url, headers=headers)
    assert over_limit.status_code == 429
    has_retry = (
        "Retry-After" in over_limit.headers
        or "X-RateLimit-Reset" in over_limit.headers
    )
    assert has_retry, "429 response missing rate-limit recovery header"

tests.name: test_export_mixed_opt_out_pii_decision_table
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role hr-admin
Directory contains employees covering all four opt-out/PII combinations: opted-out with PII, opted-out without PII, opted-in with PII, opted-in without PII
actions: Issue GET /api/employees/export.csv with hr-admin bearer token
Parse the CSV and classify each row by (opted_out, has_pii)
expected: opted_out=true rows have email and phone equal to REDACTED regardless of original PII presence
opted_out=false rows expose actual email and phone values
All directory employees appear in the CSV (no rows omitted)
tests.executable:
import csv, io
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import get_auth_token, get_all_employees_with_flags

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"
REDACT = "REDACTED"


def test_export_mixed_opt_out_pii_decision_table(<http_client>):
    token = get_auth_token(role="hr-admin")
    employees = get_all_employees_with_flags()  # list of dicts with id, opted_out, email, phone

    resp = <http_client>.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    rows = {r["employee_id"]: r for r in csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text))}
    assert len(rows) >= len(employees)

    for emp in employees:
        row = rows[emp["id"]]
        if emp["opted_out"]:
            assert row["email"] == REDACT
            assert row["phone"] == REDACT
        else:
            assert row["email"] == emp["email"]
            assert row["phone"] == emp["phone"]

tests.name: test_export_reflects_opt_out_state_transition
preconditions: Authenticated session exists for a user with role hr-admin
A known employee currently has opted_out=false
API exists to toggle an employee opt-out flag (or fixture provides direct DB toggle)
actions: Export CSV and verify employee PII is visible (opted_out=false state)
Toggle employee opt-out flag to true
Export CSV again and verify PII is redacted (opted_out=true state)
Toggle employee opt-out flag back to false
Export CSV a third time and verify PII is visible again (state restored)
expected: First export: employee row has actual email and phone
Second export: employee row has REDACTED email and phone
Third export: employee row has actual email and phone again
Fixture leaves employee in original opted_out=false state (idempotent)
tests.executable:
import csv, io
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import get_auth_token, get_opted_in_employee, set_employee_opt_out

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app"
REDACT = "REDACTED"


def _export_row(<http_client>, token, emp_id):
    resp = <http_client>.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    return {r["employee_id"]: r for r in csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text))}[emp_id]


def test_export_reflects_opt_out_state_transition(<http_client>):
    token = get_auth_token(role="hr-admin")
    emp = get_opted_in_employee()
    eid = emp["id"]

    row = _export_row(<http_client>, token, eid)
    assert row["email"] == emp["email"]

    set_employee_opt_out(eid, opted_out=True)
    try:
        row = _export_row(<http_client>, token, eid)
        assert row["email"] == REDACT
        assert row["phone"] == REDACT
    finally:
        set_employee_opt_out(eid, opted_out=False)

    row = _export_row(<http_client>, token, eid)
    assert row["email"] == emp["email"]
